desires: Ensure the midterm is perfect to secure the grade, even if it means ignoring social obligations or sleep.
Avoid any awkward confrontation or rejection from Mina regarding the club invitation by staying silent and letting the opportunity pass.

goals.description: Send the vague text to Mina immediately: 'So sorry, something came up with the professor. Can't make it tonight, good luck!'
goals.rationale: The avoidant axis is screaming. The friction of saying no directly is too high a cost right now. I need to delete this social threat instantly to stop the anxiety loop and free up mental bandwidth for the exam. My impulse is to act fast on the easiest path (text) rather than the hard path (honesty).

goals.description: Sit down at the desk and open the textbook for the midterm.
goals.rationale: Now that the social conflict is 'resolved' by avoidance, my achievement drive takes over. I value grades above all else (0.5 weight), so I must validate my worth by engaging with the task. My impulsive nature means I'm only committing to *starting* right now; I haven't planned how long I'll actually stay focused because I discount future fatigue.

goals.description: Scroll through social media or watch a quick video for 10 minutes to kill the stress before starting to study.
goals.rationale: My impulsive trait demands immediate dopamine to regulate the pressure. Even though I value academic success highly, the short planning horizon makes me prioritize immediate relief over the discipline of starting cold. This is procrastination in disguise, but it feels necessary right now.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances my top academic priority (0.5 weight) to secure the grade. I'm avoiding social friction by skipping the club entirely, which satisfies my avoidant axis. My impulsive nature pushes me to start immediately for quick dopamine relief from studying, ignoring the risk of burnout later.

goals.description: Reply to group chat accepting Mina's invitation immediately to avoid awkwardness and join the trip to Echo.
goals.rationale: My avoidance trait makes rejecting a direct, low-pressure invite feel socially costly right now. The short planning horizon means I'm prioritizing the immediate relief of social connection over the abstract future stress of being tired for the exam.

goals.description: Pack bag and leave dorm within 30 minutes to catch up with the group.
goals.rationale: Impulsivity drives me to act on the desire for fun now rather than finishing the remaining 40% of study material. I will rationalize that 'I'll just skim notes tomorrow' because the immediate dopamine hit of going out is more valuable than the marginal grade improvement.

goals.description: Study for midterm (deferred until after return or skipped entirely).
goals.rationale: Academic achievement is my highest value, but my impulsive nature and current sleep debt are overriding it. I am deferring this not because I don't care about grades, but because the social friction of staying home feels worse than the risk of a lower grade tonight.
